codeReview.summary: The feature is implemented end-to-end: React Flow graph rendering, node/edge side panels, add/edit/delete connection flows (with delete confirmation), route wiring, and focused tests. No blocking correctness defects were found in the reviewed commit.

issues.description: The page loads contacts with a fixed limit of 200, while relationships are loaded with a fixed limit of 500 in the relationships store (packages/web/src/stores/relationshipsStore.ts:25). This can silently omit nodes/edges for larger datasets, which conflicts with the feature requirement to load all contacts and connections.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The implementation-worker skill is stale for this mission context: it hardcodes org-planner paths and client/server layout that do not match the Relay mission/repo.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: org-planner/.factory/skills/implementation-worker/SKILL.md:10,18,33,41 references /Users/andy/Documents/GitHub/org-planner, packages/client, and localhost:5173, while mission AGENTS specifies /Users/andy/Documents/GitHub/relay and web port 3101 (/Users/andy/.factory/missions/e9b0e43e-24b4-4baa-87be-b21f151a466c/AGENTS.md:5,9).

sharedStateObservations.observation: The expected architecture reference document is missing from the Relay shared library, which makes architecture-boundary checks less direct during implementation/review.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: No architecture.md exists under /Users/andy/Documents/GitHub/relay/.factory/library (only user-testing.md present).

summary: Pass with one non-blocking issue: fixed page limits (200 contacts / 500 relationships) can truncate graph data in larger datasets. Also noted shared-state gaps: the implementation-worker skill is out of sync with Relay repo conventions, and architecture.md is missing from the Relay library.
